title.emphasis: Part II: Patronage as Prohibited Inducement or Kickback

section.para: In this context, "patronage" in the form of a "patronage dividend" or other consideration is treated as an illegal payment to induce referrals. These laws are designed to prevent conflicts of interest and fraud.
The Labor Code contains extensive and strict prohibitions against using patronage as kickbacks for referrals within the workers' compensation system.

listitem.para: General Prohibition (LAB §3215): It is a crime to offer or accept any "patronage... or other consideration" as an inducement for referring clients for workers' compensation services.

Prohibition on Referrals (LAB §139.3, §139.32, §3820): These sections make it unlawful for physicians or other "interested parties" (including employers, insurers, and attorneys) to offer or accept any "patronage dividend" as compensation for referrals.
Felony for Adjuster Kickbacks (LAB §3219): It is a felony to offer or accept any "patronage... or other consideration" to a claims adjuster as a reward for the referral or settlement of a claim.
Required Disclosure (LAB §4906): Attorneys in workers' compensation cases must file a statement under penalty of perjury declaring they have not accepted any "patronage dividend" for referrals.

section.para: Similar rules apply to the broader healthcare field to prevent conflicts of interest.

listitem.para: BPC §650 - Unlawful Referrals: It is unlawful for any licensed healthcare professional to offer or accept a "patronage dividend" or other consideration as a reward or "inducement for referring patients, clients, or customers."
BPC §652 - Penalties for Violation: A violation constitutes unprofessional conduct and can lead to license suspension/revocation and misdemeanor charges.

section.para: The prohibition on referral kickbacks extends to the state's disability insurance program.

listitem.para: UIC §2116 - Unlawful Inducement: It is unlawful to knowingly solicit, receive, or accept any "patronage... or other consideration" as compensation or inducement for soliciting a claimant to apply for disability insurance benefits.
